transcript.text: Hey, Alexine.
Can you hear me okay?
Oh, sorry.
think you're on mute.
I don't what's going on this morning.
Typing and Zoom pops up, it like keeps popping me on mute, both Teams and Zoom.
It's like some update happened and I hate it.
No worries.
I couldn't even tell you how many times someone starts off on mute.
It's like a rite of passage basically for, you know, using the product.
Cool, cool, cool.
Well, great to meet you.
I am excited to chat today.
Just kind of introduce you to GrowthX, learn more about how you heard of us.
Do you work with Sharon, by chance?
actually met with her from Autodesk like a week ago.
No, Sharon who?
Sharon Perdue, I think.
Oh, I'm she is.
Yeah, director of SEO over there.
Oh, that's awesome.
No.
Okay, interesting.
Cool coincidence then.
But yeah, tell me more about how you heard of us.
Yeah, so actually it was via a friend of mine who is friends with your CEO.
Okay.
Bye.
So she works at a VC, Alice Leung, at Brick and Mortar, and knows your founder really well, and was like, ooh, you guys should check these guys out.
And yeah, I'd actually, I had started, funnily enough, applying for a chief of staff role for you guys when I got my offer at Autodesk.
So I just dropped off at the process, but yeah.
So I'd like started to take a look at what you guys were doing, but yeah, it's funny.
So I come from like a peer marketing background and then got into being a chief of staff over the last four or five years now, which has been a really fun learning curve.
But very weirdly, a marketing challenge has now dropped in my lap, which I thought I was done with those days, where we had a lot of layoffs of product marketeers in the Autodesk team.
So we are looking to have almost like a guerrilla marketing organization within my organization.
So I'll be a program manager running either cloud tractors.
Generating content or looking at a solution like yours, there's, you know, several question marks that spread into my mind.
Like, what we have handle on the social media channels because we're not part of the official marketing side of the org.
Why can't we just do email marketing?
Because we're Autodesk and we already have the emails of everybody we need.
But I digress.
Yeah, so that's the one way we can get around it is by using contracting dollars.
So right now we're sort of slapping together between the leadership team, a very ad hoc, I wouldn't even call it a strategy, ad hoc social posts, ad hoc blogs, things like that.
And our industry strategy partner is great at, you know, pulling clients in for webinars and things like that.
But it is not cohesive whatsoever.
And it sucks.
So we're basically the data model part of AC.
So architecture, engineering, construction.
Yeah, we have a host of really cool things.
can do with your data if you're an Autodesk customer.
And when our industry strategy guide goes on the road, first question he always asks is like, how many people know that we have a data model?
And it's always like three to 10 people out of 100 or 200, which is ridiculous.
So we have an awareness problem, a marketeer's dream.
How do you even measure that?
But yeah, so that's where we are.
And I'd love to hear more about like how you guys could help.
I'm not sure if we're quite yet at the price point.
I have like eight grand a month all-in budget USD.
So I'm not sure.
Like I had seen after I booked this call that you guys start at 15.
So I'm not sure what's possible.
But yeah, very curious about what you guys offer.
Yeah, definitely.

And I think, you know, my conversation with Sharon, she was like more interested in the strategy side of our offering as opposed to like us being an extension of the content team and creating content, you know, on Autodesk's behalf.
Mm But...
With that said, she was like open to how we could execute on the content side since there's like no shortage of content needs, right?
Like, you know, you said it yourself, there's so many different products, so many different teams involved.
So I guess, what do you know about growthx since you do have some familiarity with who we are from that open position?
A ton, to be honest.
mean, it looks like it's AI, but with a team of specialists kind of running the AI applications.
I don't know, it looks like you guys do a mix from like SEO and strategy type work to the content, but like how that magic is made, no idea.
Yeah, for sure.
And I'll go under the hood today.
But I guess just to take like a quick step back and introduce myself, I'm the founding AE here at GrowthX.
I'm, you know, based in Houston, but traveling to our office in San Francisco this week, which is always a lot of fun.
And, you know, what we do at GrowthX, we're really like an end-to-end partner.
Where we'll help you think about your, you know, SEO and GEO strategy, and then we'll go in and actually execute content daily.
So, you know, we'll create one article per day.
It could be a net new article or a refreshed existing piece.

And we typically are doing editorial as like a foundation, but then we'll move into different lanes like a programmatic SEO or, you know, for some clients, we'll create like a newsletter on their behalf.
And then we also just help you see how you're showing up with AI search and monitor and help you make, you know, improvements on that on like a weekly or bi-weekly basis.
So I guess what, you know, in terms of your part of the business, like who's creating content today and is, you know, what you need really just more bandwidth and more execution power?
At the moment, I would say probably yes.
Unfortunately, I don't think there's, there's only so far.
We can get in terms of strategy, like we really need to treat our department almost as like a company on its own, which sucks and is really like not holistic.
But like what Sharon's after is going to be something completely different from what we're after.
Like an overarching engagement with GrowthX would be something very, very different and very cool.
And there's definitely a few people I could talk to about that, but I don't think that would be like a short term thing.
Let's put it that way.
It's very much like for the AAC data model team that we would need that right now.
Okay.
And I guess help me understand that better, right?
Like I know what kind of products you guys make, but like help me just better understand your team within this ecosystem.
This is literally the next article we're working on.
So I was like, this is clear as mud right now.
So basically, I didn't know you had a data model either.
So yeah.
Yeah, no, all good.
Opening up API access to certain things like extensibility of data, the ability to
granularize data.
So one of the biggest problems in AEC is model size.
Like they're so big and so heavy.
And you also really want to restrict access to the models so people don't mess it up.
So if you have folks who don't need to be in the model but need like room data or whatnot, the ability to extract that without going into the model is huge.
Or say they just need to look at like one wall and all the things on that wall.
Instead of opening up the entire thing, they can have just that granular data, including granular data from a geometry point of view.
So not just the information in it, but also, you know, the visual.
Hey, that wasn't bad for someone who's only been here six months.
Yeah.
It doesn't work in marketing anymore.
No, that was great.
So I guess like who are the practical users of this data model?
Is it like architects, engineers, consultants, or more broader, like someone running an entire project?
It varies a lot.
But we could definitely come up with a list of like job titles that could be interesting.
That being said,
You know, I haven't quite decided if we want to go with targeted ads or whatnot yet, because that would have to fit within the A-K budget.
So, yeah, not sure how far we could go there, but it really is a mix.
can be people who are either really very high-tech or very low-tech.
They're so high-tech, they want to save the time of going in the model, or they're so low-tech, they don't know how to go in the model.
And, you know, they want, like, the easy button.
And we have, you know, SDKs for companies that have their own teams of developers and just want to pull what they want to pull into their data lake and do what they want to do there.
Whereas other ones, you know, we have, like, pre-done, like the easy button, joke.

But, like, for instance, like, extensibility of certain properties, well, there's a way to do that in a button in the software that you can go ahead and click and do that instead of writing your own code, basically.
Okay, that makes sense.
And so are these, like...

Folks who access the data model, like, are they existing Autodesk Enterprise customers, or are they, and you're just trying to help spread awareness on, like, more value they could get from the suite of products?
It's not like you guys are going out and trying to get net new visitors for this.
It's all existing customers that this...
Yep.
There's upsell opportunities with some of our products that are, you know, within the data model.
But, yeah, it's existing Autodesk clients.
So, again, this is one of the things that's driving me crazy.
Like, we have those...
Like, nine tenths of the battle is usually getting net new contacts.
But, you know, we have that database of existing people.
But I know Autodesk is very strict on how they market to existing users about...
They're allowed to market about specific products, and that's pretty much it.
Like, we can make an argument for some of these things going on top of certain products and hopefully get some marketing value there.
But that's not the main focus of those teams either.
Um...
So yeah, it's a weird challenge.
Yeah, I hear you.
And I definitely want to dig in on our side.
I'm just trying to sort of figure out like where our services might fit in because this is definitely more unique.
Like it's typically clients want to use GrowthX so that they can show up in AI search for net new prospects.
Totally, totally.
Like if I was at a startup still, like this is my first like corporate gig.
Like I've always been at startups and I'm like, you know, the typical startup, they want, they want their logo recognized.
want to like define a category.
They want to like, and I, I could see that like big strategy being a huge part of what you guys do.
And it's really hard to define this.
Yeah.
What kind of content would you be looking for a partner to help you create?
Blog posts, social content, thought leadership, blog posts, that type of thing.
Yeah.
Blog, social, thought leadership.
And would it live on like the auto.
At one of the Autodesk websites, or where would it live?
Yes.
So it would still need to go through our internal validation process and legal review and all that jazz.
Right.
Right.
And so who creates this content today?
Do you have a small team of content creators, or is it just the contractors that you mentioned?
Yeah, contractors.
So we have one contractor part-time, and then it's the rest of the industry strategy team and some of our product managers trying to write the content themselves.
Okay.
And I guess, where are you at today with scale?
Because you're trying to create more, right?
You're trying to have more bandwidth through execution partners.
Like, how many blog posts are you doing today, and what would you want to get to?
We're doing like one a month right now.
Okay.
And then what's the goal?
Well, it depends if it's a person doing it or, say, I doing it.
My goals would be very different.
I mean, weekly would be amazing, regardless of either.
Weekly would be awesome.
There's also an opportunity for...
I think more like thought leadership content.
So we've had some customer cohorts where they've joined us the day before our leadership team meetings in the last two quarters in different locations around the world that we've done.
And they're really hungry for people to like tell them how to set up their information requirements, for instance, or data standardization and classification.
And we have like the people that are in that room are the ones on the bleeding edge of data.
And they're still saying it would be helpful if there was something to point to and say, this is how it should be done.
And if Autodesk could leave that charge, I mean, they think Autodesk should solve all their problems.
I think we'd solve some of them.

So there's definitely an opportunity for some thought leadership type content there too, which can potentially live on like, you know, our senior directors, LinkedIn, or I don't know, there could be a bunch of different options there.
Definitely, because what I'm trying to sort of navigate and understand is with the content, you know, how much of it is like...
Information about using the product versus information about what it unlocks, right?
Because we wouldn't necessarily, at the beginning, create content that would be content your product experts would put out there for product adoption, right?
For sure, for sure.
And we do have like separate teams that do like support content and stuff like that.
And, you know, I can see like new features and things like that still sitting with our product teams.
It's not that much skin off their back to, you know, put the information and they've already put a million other places in another one.
But I think that's sort of like why it benefits and driving the industry to a stronger data strategy happens to use the AC data model.
But that kind of thought leadership and why this matters and a little bit of how you can go about doing it would be really helpful.
Right, right.
More like, especially for the novice user, like the non-high tech person, like why is there a benefit?
And that's 95% of our industry, like, you know, it's, it's that next, that next phase.
And there's a lot of, there's a huge appetite for it.
Anytime we do like a class at one of our, one of our shows in industry, it's always absolutely packed out.
Like, we're talking 1,300 people registered at a 10,000 person event, and only 500 were allowed in.
Yeah.
Like, like, they're, yeah, people know they need to be more data savvy, but how they get there.
I think that needs some nurturing.
Okay, understood.
And last question, I swear, what's, what's getting in the way of like, the team who's creating the content today, being able to, you know, do this for you?
Like, why do you need a part of growth acts or someone else?
Totally, totally bandwidth.
It's like, not their, one of their most important priorities.
It's like, something that's just on their responsibilities.
We don't have a marketing team to do it, except for one part time.
And she's mostly the one running it through everything.
it's happening on top of everyone else's normal jobs.
Okay, got it.
Yeah, I think it would definitely be helpful to see a couple examples, whether you want to like share them in the chat or share them after, no big deal.
Yeah.
But thank you for all this context.
Let me give you some more insight into like how we work at GrowthX.
I think it could be a fit, but it's probably going to be connected to the existing conversation that I'm having, which could work well, think, like given the budget that you have, right?
The 8K versus the 15K, which is the starting price.
But I digress.
So let me share a couple slides and give you some more context.
Yeah.
So yeah, I mentioned like the end-to-end nature of our partnership and a lot of clients, pretty much everyone uses us to show up in AI search.
And so I think if there's a need for that, definitely could be a fit.
If there's also just a need to have resources that your team could send to these existing clients, I can see some benefit there.
But Alexine, our goal is to help you show up in AI search because the first sales conversation happens there.
If you're in the answer, people hit the website with a lot more likelihood to convert all the great stuff you've been hearing.
And so, you know, our clients, Monograph, we help on both the traditional and the AI search realm.
I know Monograph.
Oh, nice.
Yeah.
It's a small world.
Figured you might know them.
Yeah, that makes a ton of sense.
And the whole idea is just that, you know, our founder and CEO, he's built organic content engines at every step in his career.
He's always believed brands should act more like publishers.
And so, you know, whether it was HashiCorp when they went from six to 100 million or being the CMO at scale for the acquisition by Meta, this is just...
It's like a tried and true tested part of, you know, his marketing leadership.
And then at DeepGram, what he did is he replicated the process of like building this massive team by automating where he could with AI workflows.
And so it's this combination of really sophisticated AI workflows, which I'll show you, and then experts in the loop for each step of the process.
Any just like high level questions for me so far?
No.
Cool.
And so, you know, what we do is we typically start off with this eight-week strategy sprint, and then we move into a 12-month growth execution.
And so when we're creating content, we're using this internal platform that we built that we're in the process of, you know, bringing clients more into.
And then the experts who would help create the content, you know, they're folks who come from like SEO and content marketing backgrounds.
Our chief content officer, he was the editor-in-chief of TechCrunch.
For over a decade.
so the idea is you are getting the best of both approaches.
And we work with a lot of clients who are pretty technical.

So one thing that we're able to do is we can do a lot of context engineering and the strategy spread to develop these artifacts that helps us create content that's more relevant to your ICP and is written in your brand voice.
And so that's like some of our process of like how we write content that your team, right, would be able to create in the style, you know, that you want the content written.
And so, you know, what we would do is in the strategy sprint, we would focus on generating those artifacts and then having the roadmap that we would execute on for the next six months.
And then in phase two, we would actually go in and try to publish, right, every single day.
I think in this use case, it would be more like publishing.
Weekly, just because we're not creating content that's for SEO purposes, right?
We're creating content that's more for, like, product awareness.
But it sounds like you're looking for, like, one or two blogs per week.
Yeah.
And then the other thing is we don't typically create content for social media.
So how important is that to the use case here?
We're still figuring it out.
Okay, just more, like, exploratory right now.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay.
So I think to get you, like, some ideas, one thing that we've done is we've created all these guides for Augmented Code.
So a lot of these guides, they're somewhat thought leadership, you know, I would say more so, like, comparing tools in this space, you know, giving recommendations for, like, AI coding.
But what's nice about this content is that, you know, we do everything from the image generation.
image generation.
everything generation.
We We
To cross-linking, to different pieces on your website, to even having these code snippets built into the content.
So we can get pretty custom on what we create.
That's very cool.
Yeah, like I was Googling, you know, how can I work with my data better in AEC?
And like the AI overview is all about like how to prioritize data quality and governance with links to it, you know?
Haven't gone through to see how many links those are to us, but like that would be really cool.
Yeah, I mean, do you see any citations to Autodesk on those searches?
I do, actually.
So someone's doing their job somewhere.
And I guess, do you know like what team is creating that content?
Because it sounds like that's the kind of content that you're looking to create, right?
It's just like a different part of the organization.
That's it.
So actually one goes straight to our homepage.
So someone's done a great job on SEO there and that's at the top.
Yeah.
So yeah, it sounds like we're getting there, but I think there's a lot of...
Whether it necessarily needs, you know, the big all-singing, all-dancing effort, like growthx can deliver or not, I'm still not sure.
Maybe not at this stage, but we are growing very quickly.
We are a platform, and I believe this is the year that we truly become a platform team with regards to the rest of Autodesk.
And we start having tons more integrations and things like that.
And as, you know, the team size get bigger, so does the budget.
So, it may be just a not yet, but this is really, really cool.
I love what you guys are doing.
No, I appreciate that.
And I think for, like, our larger clients, we do a lot of refresh because we recommend, like, every three months you should refresh content.
So, like, Ramp, you know, we've done some more custom projects like this vendor directory where, you know, Ramp wants to be the authority for anything business spending.
So, we've got, like, a page for all of the most popular tools, and then we've programmatically...
We brought Ramp's spend data into each one of these pages.
And so this is like an interesting thing we've done for Ramp that came a bit later in the partnership.
We've also done like these per diem calculators.
So there's a page if you're looking up the per diem in any city in America.
That's awesome.

And then we've even done like this expense category classifier where if someone was to search, you know, like what expense category is office furniture or charitable donations, there's a page that's, you know, optimized for SEO and GEO that we created for Ramp in each one of those scenarios.

So I think, you know, whether it's more pure play editorial, these examples from Airbyte are probably a good reference as well because it's a lot of like data engineering resources, similar to what you're talking about.
Or even something that's a little bit more custom, like, you know, Webflow, we've created their integrations.
And docs pages, there's probably a place for us to fit in.
We just have to define with existing examples.
And also, I think maybe with like more stakeholders, like where could we make the base impact?
And I guess like what efforts outside of content are you guys trying today with, you know, some of your customers who aren't maybe leveraging like the data model as much as they should?
Yeah, that's it.
And it's just a bit hard because we're operating outside of the existing marketing team.
Like what keys are we going to have access to and what won't we have access to?
Whereas content is fairly easy because we can just put it through their process and then they can go put it wherever it needs to be done.
You know, something more advanced is going to require more closer integration with the marketing team.
We do have an ask for more dedicated employees that should be approved hopefully early February.
We're not holding our breath, but that could make a big difference too because if we have someone who can give us access to these things.
have difference.
So I could see this being more appropriate.
For sure.
But yeah, I think, you know, as a next step, I'm happy to go into more of like a demo of how we create content.
You could, I know you had someone else on the invite.
You're welcome to loop them back in, or I can also connect with Sharon as a next step and see if it makes sense to get more of like a group demo.
Yeah.
Whatever you think would be best.
But yeah, we do do like the 30K strategy spread and then the 15K per month growth execution.
Yeah.
So we probably need to figure out like who else would benefit from the content creation that we would do before going too deep into your use case alone.
Totally.
If you agree.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And I think based on what you're saying right now, like, I don't think this is a right now spend for us.
It's too much outside of our budget.
That being said, if Sharon has the budget to do that within her team, I'd be happy to just sit like a fly on the wall in the demo of the content.
I just want to be mindful of your guys' time.

Um, I'd love to know when we switch from a contractor strategy to something more like this, but I need to understand more how we can get our, you know, the keys to all the marketing that already exists.
You know what I mean?
I feel like without that, it's just not going to be as powerful as what you guys could potentially do.
For sure.
And, um, I guess like with the 8K that, you know, you're looking to find a partner for, what specifically are you looking for?
Is it a partner to create more content and you need to spend those funds this fiscal year or is there some flexibility on the timeline?
It's really to create content.
Um, we have the budget to ask in for 25K per quarter for all of next year.
Um, we do have the opportunity to usually ask for contingency budget requests, you know.
Partway throughout the year, particularly around sort of like June, July.
So it could be a good time for us to reconnect and kind of see where we're at.
Um, cause I'd love to understand how we could eventually go from like.
A contractor model to something like this.
I think it's just a lot more holistic and data forward.
Yeah, I think I think what could work well, you know, if that next conversation with Sharon, if we feel like there's a lane on the execution side for them, not just strategy.
I think that your group could benefit from our strategy sprint where we can help you see what are the different content opportunities that we could actually go out and execute on.
And then, yeah, it'd be interesting to see what what they come up with.
I will say, like, Autodesk very much operates like, this is my money, this is your money.
Okay, okay.
Unfortunately, yeah, yeah.
Okay.
But I'll keep you looped in because I think that you probably wouldn't have to spend anything to benefit from the strategy sprint where you'd see those opportunities and get some calibration pieces.
That's true.
And I'll reach out to Sharon directly and just introduce myself and say, by the way, I was looking at them too.
You know, it's a bit bit out of our budget for now.
But if you continue conversations with them, I'd love.
That's perfect, yeah, because I definitely see a world where your team can just benefit from like one of those pieces being per week being focused on your use case, right?
Like the other four could be on the use case that they find.
In an ideal world, that would be wonderful.
But yeah, reach out to Sharon as a first step and I'll let you know how the conversation with her goes to see if there's like some appetite for cross-pollinating, right?
Like some of the work that we do.
I think she's all over, so there actually could be.
I need to check exactly what she's doing, but...
She mentioned that, that there's like several teams that she creates content for, so I think that, yeah.
God, if that's our content team, that is hecka small.
I'm just looking at it and aware.
But anyways, it's good to meet you.
I got to run to do an interview with somebody, but pleasure making the intro and I'll send you some resources so you can kind of chat internally.
I more to figure out where
Yeah, makes sense.
Really appreciate your time.
Thanks so much.
And good luck with your interview.
Thanks.
Have a great week.
Bye.
You too.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Intro call to assess GrowthX's fit for Autodesk's AEC data model team.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=261.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Problem:** The AEC data model team has a major awareness problem among existing Autodesk customers (only 3–10% are aware), caused by a lack of marketing bandwidth.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
  - [**Budget Mismatch:** The team's budget ($8k/mo now, $25k/qtr next year) is far below GrowthX's starting price of $15k/mo for execution.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot:** A standalone engagement is unfeasible. The focus shifted to exploring a joint project with Sharon Perdue's SEO team, which is already in talks with GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1722.0)
  - [**Path Forward:** Lake will discuss execution support with Sharon. If viable, Alexine's team could get content from the strategy sprint at no cost and potentially contribute to a larger contract.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1806.0)

## Topics

### Autodesk's AEC Data Model: The Awareness Gap

  - [**Team:** Alexine's team manages the AEC data model, a platform providing granular data access (e.g., room data, single walls) without opening heavy BIM models.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [**Audience:** Existing Autodesk customers, ranging from high-tech developers (using SDKs) to low-tech users needing simple buttons.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=702.0)
  - [**Core Challenge:** A severe awareness gap. Only 3–10% of customers know the data model exists, despite high demand for related topics (e.g., data standardization) at industry events.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)

### The Content & Marketing Bottleneck

  - [**Current State:** Content creation is ad-hoc, handled by product managers and one part-time contractor.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=911.0)
  - [**Volume:** Only \~1 blog post per month.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=928.0)
  - [**Goal:** Increase output to weekly blogs and thought leadership content.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=928.0)
  - [**Constraint:** The team operates outside the official marketing org, limiting access to channels like email and social media. Content must be funded via contracting dollars.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=335.0)

### GrowthX's Solution & Budget Mismatch

  - [**GrowthX's Model:** An end-to-end partner combining AI workflows with human experts to produce daily content for AI search.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Phase 1:** 8-week strategy sprint ($30k).](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1704.0)
      - [**Phase 2:** 12-month execution ($15k/mo).](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1704.0)
  - [**Budget Conflict:** The AEC team's budget ($8k/mo now, $25k/qtr next year) is insufficient for a standalone engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [**Service Mismatch:** The AEC team needs thought leadership for existing customers, while GrowthX's core model is for net-new customer acquisition via AI search.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)

### The Path Forward: A Joint Project

  - [**Opportunity:** A potential joint project with Sharon Perdue's SEO team, which is already in talks with GrowthX for strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1693.0)
  - [**Rationale:** A larger, cross-departmental contract could absorb the AEC team's budget and provide the execution support they need.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1710.0)
  - [**Benefit for AEC Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1806.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Gain insights from the strategy sprint at no cost.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1806.0)
      - [**Execution:** Potentially receive weekly content for the data model use case.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1862.0)
  - [**Constraint:** Autodesk's "my money, your money" culture could complicate budget sharing.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1829.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Lake:**](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1850.0)
      - [Send GrowthX resources to Alexine.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1908.0)
      - [Contact Sharon Perdue to discuss adding execution support for the AEC data model team to a potential contract.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1806.0)
  - [**Alexine:**](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1850.0)
      - [Connect with Sharon Perdue to express interest in a joint GrowthX engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/85UGZeSXv3gSXFxzDKeQskc5G66WUiLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1850.0)
